SEARCH

Class Person

java.lang.Object
Person
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
Direct Known Subclasses:
Patient
public abstract class Person extends Object implements Serializable
Abstract base class for all people tracked by the emergency dispatch system.

Centralises the attributes shared by every person in the domain model: a unique identifier, a display name and a geographic position. Concrete subclasses (e.g. Patient) extend it to add role-specific data.

Implementing Serializable allows any subclass instance to be persisted and restored by MapPersistenceService without additional configuration.

Equality is based solely on the identifier so that two references to the same logical person compare as equal regardless of positional changes.

Version:
1.0
Author:
Maissa Tirsane, Anas Chokri, Iyed Souissi, Valery Vo-Van

  • Field Details

    • id

      protected String id
      Unique identifier that distinguishes this person from all others in the system. Declared protected so that subclasses can read it directly in overridden toString methods without going through a getter.

    • name

      protected String name
      Human-readable display name shown in the graphical interface and console output.

    • position

      protected Coordinate position
      Current geographic position of this person on the dispatch map.

  • Constructor Details

    • Person

      public Person(String id, String name, Coordinate position)
      Initialises the three attributes shared by all person subclasses.
      Parameters:
      id - unique identifier; must be non-null and non-blank
      name - display name shown to the dispatcher
      position - initial geographic position on the map

  • Method Details

    • getPosition

      public Coordinate getPosition()
      Returns the current geographic position of this person.
      Returns:
      current position; never null after construction

    • updatePosition

      public void updatePosition(Coordinate newPosition)
      Moves this person to a new geographic position.

      Called whenever the dispatcher drags a person on the map or issues a move command from the CLI. MapManager triggers a full recomputation after calling this method so that assignments and geometry remain consistent.

      Parameters:
      newPosition - the destination position; must not be null

    • getId

      public String getId()
      Returns the unique identifier of this person.
      Returns:
      identifier string; never null

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns the display name of this person.
      Returns:
      name string; never null

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
